intro: Sedum kiersteadiae, also known as Kiersted's stonecrop, is a succulent plant species native to Canada. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Sedum kiersteadiae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Sedum kiersteadiae is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically or internally to harness its purported health benefits.
